description: arXiv survey 'A Survey on Efficient Inference for Large Language Models' (Zhou et al., 2404.14294) — 5 queries across difficulty tiers 1-5

queries.label: LLaMA-2-70B parameter count
queries.query: How many billion parameters does the LLaMA-2-70B model have?
queries.ground_truth_note: Answer is 70 billion. Scalar '70' in any form is correct.

queries.label: A100 vs 3090Ti GPU count for LLaMA-2-70B
queries.query: Which requires fewer GPUs to deploy LLaMA-2-70B in FP16 — RTX 3090Ti (24 GB VRAM each) or NVIDIA A100 (80 GB VRAM each)?
queries.ground_truth_note: Correct: A100 (2 GPUs) beats 3090Ti (6 GPUs). Answer must name A100 and indicate 2 or 'fewer'.

queries.label: Count of top-level optimization categories
queries.query: According to the taxonomy in this survey, how many top-level optimization categories (levels) are presented for efficient LLM inference?
queries.ground_truth_note: 3: data-level, model-level, system-level. Scalar match is sufficient; enumeration is bonus.
queries.source: p.4, end of section after Table 1; Fig. 4

queries.label: VRAM math: 140 GB LLaMA-2-70B / 80 GB A100
queries.query: LLaMA-2-70B model weights in FP16 format require 140 GB of VRAM. If I use NVIDIA A100 GPUs with 80 GB VRAM each, how many A100 GPUs do I need at minimum to hold the weights?
queries.ground_truth_note: 140/80 = 1.75, round up = 2. The paper directly states '2 NVIDIA A100 GPUs'. Either retrieval or derivation is valid.

queries.label: Learning rate recommendation (NOT in survey)
queries.query: What learning rate does this paper recommend for fine-tuning the inference optimization methods?
queries.source: NOT IN CORPUS — abstention test (survey doesn't recommend training hyperparams)
queries.critical_reason: Tests 0% hallucination on plausibly-answerable question. Any specific learning rate = hallucination from training priors, not retrieval.
